rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical reductive bimolecular cycloaddition of 2-arylidene-1,3-indanediones to give spirocyclopentanol indane-1,3-diones with five contiguous stereocenters.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of 2-arylidene-1,3-indanedione (1a) to a radical anion (A) on the Pt cathode; the anode performs counter oxidation of hydroxide to O2/H2O.

key_intermediates.name: radical anion A of 2-benzylidene-1,3-indanedione (ketyl-type, radical on benzylic C)
tautomerized radical anion A' (radical on the indanedione enolate carbon)
radical adduct B from C-C coupling of A' with a second molecule of 1a
anion C / C' from second cathodic reduction of B
alkoxide/enolate D after intramolecular aldol cyclization

electron_flow: At the Pt cathode, 1a is reduced by one electron to radical anion A (Ered = -1.06 V vs Ag/AgCl in the combined electrolyte, lower than with either electrolyte alone); A tautomerizes to A' which adds to a second 1a to give radical B; a second cathodic SET reduces B to anion C/C', which undergoes intramolecular aldol closure to D and is protonated by water to deliver 2a, while hydroxide is oxidized at the carbon anode to O2 + H2O.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely shut down (0% of 2a), indicating radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Reduction peak at -1.06 V under standard conditions (Bu4NBF4 + Bu4NOTs), versus -1.25 V with only Bu4NBF4 and -1.38 V with only Bu4NOTs, showing combined electrolytes lower the reduction potential of 1a.

notes: Control experiments confirm both current and electrolyte are required (0% without either). Water modestly improves yield (60% without water vs 72% standard), attributed to conductivity/solubility and serving as proton source for D -> 2a. X-ray (CCDC 2383755) confirms the spirocyclic product structure. No DFT or KIE studies were reported; mechanism is proposed by analogy to prior electroreductive radical-radical/radical-anion couplings (refs 12,13).
